title: podman network create of unmanaged network should allow overlap with host networks
body:
### Issue Description

```
[root@21a40ff9fb76 /]# podman version
Client:        Podman Engine
Version:       5.4.1
API Version:   5.4.1
Go Version:    go1.23.7
Git Commit:    b79bc8afe796cba51dd906270a7e1056ccdfcf9e
Built:         Tue Mar 11 00:00:00 2025
Build Origin:  Fedora Project
OS/Arch:       linux/amd64
```

I would like to deploy containers on an existing bridge managed by the sysadmin (in production it will be created by a systemd netdev unit, but in this issue I create it with a shell script).
The documentation of podman-network-create suggests that with -omode=unmanaged I ought to be able to do that.
I deployed a podman:latest container and ran the following shell script:
```
#!/bin/bash
set -e
dnf install iproute
ip link add aces type bridge
ip addr add 192.168.33.3/24 dev aces
podman network create aces --interface-name=aces -omode=unmanaged --subnet=192.168.33.0/24 --gateway 192.168.33.254 --ip-range 192.168.33.10-192.168.33.20 --disable-dns
```
But I get the following error in response to the podman network create command:
```
Error: subnet 192.168.33.0/24 is already used on the host or by another config
```
I think this is a bug because the entire point of creating an unmanaged network is that the network will already exist by the time any container is run on it.
I can suppress the error by running `ip link set aces down` before the podman network create and then bringing the link back up later.
That's hard to do if my network is being created by a netdev unit.
I think that the check for overlapping host configurations should be suppressed for a unmanaged network. Or if more resources are available, then exclude the interface where the network is being deployed from the test.


### Steps to reproduce the issue

```
podman run -ti --rm --privileged -vnetwork.sh:/network.sh podman:latest
/network.sh
```

### Describe the results you received

Describe the results you received
`Error: subnet 192.168.33.0/24 is already used on the host or by another config
`

### Describe the results you expected

Describe the results you expected
I expected the network to be created.

### Podman in a container

Yes

### Privileged Or Rootless

Privileged

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

I've reproduced the issue outside of a container; it was just easier to give a reproduction test case using podman in podman.

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting
